prompt:
I'm sick of every ingest script pairing encoding/csv with 40 lines of hand-written column-index bookkeeping that silently breaks when the partner reorders columns. I want a small decoder that goes straight from CSV bytes to structs by HEADER NAME. And because we need to report precise positions to the partner when their feed is malformed (and later relax quoting rules encoding/csv won't let us relax), parsing has to be our own — do not import encoding/csv.

Package csvdec, root of module go-csv-decode, stdlib only. Two exported functions:

  func ParseRecords(data []byte) ([][]string, error)
  func Unmarshal(data []byte, v any) error   // v must be a *[]T where T is a struct

Parsing rules (RFC-4180-ish): comma-separated fields; records end at \n, and \r\n is accepted without leaking the \r into values; a final record without trailing newline still counts; completely blank lines are skipped. Quoted fields: wrap in double quotes, doubled "" is a literal quote, and quoted fields may contain commas AND newlines. Malformed input — an unterminated quote, or a bare quote in the middle of an unquoted field — is an error whose message includes the 1-based line number, like "line 2". Empty input parses to zero records.

Mapping rules for Unmarshal: the first record is the header (no header at all = error). A struct field maps to the column named by its `csv:"name"` tag; tag "-" opts out entirely; untagged exported fields match their own name case-insensitively. Supported field types: string, int, float64, bool — a matching column on any other type is an error naming the struct field. Extra CSV columns are ignored; struct fields with no matching column stay zero. Every data row must have exactly as many fields as the header (error names the line). Conversion failures name both the column and the line, e.g. bad int in "hits" on line 3 should mention hits and line 3. Rows with wrong destination types (nil, non-pointer, *int, *[]int) are errors, not panics.

csvdec_test.go is the acceptance contract — hands off. go test -race -timeout 30s ./... green means done.
